title: Inconsistent 'x handling in opt_expr for muxes
body:
### Version

0.51+107

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

uut.il
```
module \uut
  wire output 1 \out
  wire input 1 \a
  wire input 1 \b
  cell $mux \mux
    parameter \WIDTH 1
    connect \A \a
    connect \B \b
    connect \S 1'x
    connect \Y \out
  end
end
```
Call `yosys -p 'opt_expr -mux_undef [-keepdc]; dump' uut.il`

For reference:
`yosys -p 'opt_expr; dump' uut.il` has `out` directly connected to `a`.
`yosys -p 'opt_expr -keepdc; dump' uut.il` is unchanged.

### Expected Behavior

`yosys -p 'opt_expr -mux_undef' uut.il` shouldn't connect `out` to `a`, I would have expected it to connect `out` to `1'x` (which is what happens when both `A` and `B` are undef), but I'm not entirely sure.
`yosys -p 'opt_expr -mux_undef -keepdc; dump' uut.il` should be unchanged.

### Actual Behavior

`opt_expr -mux_undef` with or without `-keepdc` gives
```
module \uut
  wire input 1 \b
  wire input 2 \a
  wire output 3 \out
  connect \out \a
end
```
i.e. the same behaviour as `opt_expr` without `-keepdc`, completely ignoring the x bits..
code_blocks:
module \uut
  wire output 1 \out
  wire input 1 \a
  wire input 1 \b
  cell $mux \mux
    parameter \WIDTH 1
    connect \A \a
    connect \B \b
    connect \S 1'x
    connect \Y \out
  end
end

module \uut
  wire input 1 \b
  wire input 2 \a
  wire output 3 \out
  connect \out \a
end
